page: Microsoft Azure D48alds_v6 is a virtualized server instance running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It is powered by an AMD EPYC 9V74 processor utilizing the x86_64 architecture, configured with 48 dedicated vCPUs across 24 physical cores with multithreading enabled. The CPU operates at a base clock speed of 3.7 GHz and features advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 and cryptography acceleration. The instance is equipped with 96.0 GB of system memory, establishing a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per vCPU, alongside 2835 GB of bundled local NVMe storage.

Performance benchmarks position this instance in the top tier (top 10%) for Geekbench multi-core execution and small-model LLM text generation. It demonstrates strong (top 25%) performance in stress-ng CPU tests, cached memory bandwidth, software compilation, and Redis SET operations. Conversely, uncached memory bandwidth and static web serving throughput fall into the average range. Due to the lack of dedicated GPU hardware and the constrained memory footprint, large-scale LLM text generation using a 70B model is weak, representing a clear performance tradeoff for AI workloads.

The D48alds_v6 offers a highly cost-efficient profile for compute-intensive workloads that do not require extensive memory allocations. By pairing a high-frequency 48-vCPU processor with a modest 96.0 GB RAM configuration, it minimizes memory overhead costs while maximizing raw processing throughput. This resource balance, combined with substantial local storage, makes the instance well-suited for multi-threaded CPU tasks, software compilation, medium-scale database operations, and localized web serving, whereas memory-bound applications are less optimal.
description: The Microsoft Azure D48alds_v6 is an x86_64 virtual server powered by an AMD EPYC 9V74 processor running at 3.7 GHz. It features 48 dedicated vCPUs, 96.0 GB of RAM, and 2835 GB of local storage. Operating on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this instance delivers strong multi-core CPU performance, ranking in the top 10% for Geekbench multi-core benchmarks. With a low memory-to-vCPU ratio of 2.0 GB per core, the server provides a cost-efficient hardware profile for compute-heavy tasks. It excels in software compilation, multi-threaded decompression, and Redis operations, though its limited memory footprint makes it less suitable for large-scale in-memory databases or heavy LLM text generation.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D48alds_v6 is an x86_64 server with 48 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 96 GB RAM, and 2835 GB local storage, delivering top-tier multi-core performance for compute-heavy workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D48alds_v6 features 48 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 96 GB RAM, and 2835 GB local storage, optimized for high-compute, low-memory workloads.
tagline: A high-compute virtual server featuring 48 AMD EPYC vCPUs, fast local storage, and optimized resource density for processing-heavy workloads.
bullet_points: AMD EPYC 9V74 processor with 48 dedicated vCPUs and 3.7 GHz clock speed
96.0 GB of system memory providing 2.0 GB of RAM per vCPU
2835 GB of bundled local NVMe storage for high-speed data access
Top-tier multi-core performance in Geekbench and bzip3 decompression
Optimized for software compilation, Redis operations, and compute-heavy tasks
